chatgpt.reasoning_summary: Loading remote URL minimizes build complexity and keeps parity with web app; local assets add complexity and risk for phase 1. Remote mode allows quick iteration and easier auth flow handling.
chatgpt.auth_v1_recommendation: Use email code login and OAuth via external browser with deep linking for safe, minimal changes.
must_change_now: Implement deep link handling for OAuth redirects
Configure Capacitor Browser plugin for OAuth flows
Update API client base URL to absolute remote URL
Add Capacitor App plugin event listeners for lifecycle
Ensure CORS and credentials work with remote origin
Add minimal native splash screen and app metadata
can_defer_until_next_round: Package local web assets for offline support
main_risks: CORS and credential issues with remote origin
User experience lag on initial remote load
Potential mismatch between web and native behaviors
implementation_order: Set Capacitor shell to load remote URL
Integrate Capacitor Browser plugin for OAuth flows
Implement deep link listeners with Capacitor App plugin
Adjust API client to use absolute remote URL with credentials
Test email code login and OAuth flows end-to-end
Prepare minimal native app metadata and splash screen

grok.reasoning_summary: Local assets minimize latency and auth redirect friction while keeping the web codebase untouched. Remote-first adds unnecessary CORS and session risks for a v1 native wrapper.
grok.auth_v1_recommendation: Keep email code login as primary path and gate OAuth behind external browser for v1.
must_change_now: Make API base URL configurable via env
Add App plugin URL listener for redirects
Replace window.location with Capacitor Browser where possible
main_risks: OAuth state loss on app resume
implementation_order: Init Capacitor and sync web build

gemini.reasoning_summary: Loading local assets first provides a more stable, faster, and predictable user experience for the initial release, minimizing network dependencies and app store review complexities. This approach aligns with low-risk delivery and a practical base for future evolution.
gemini.auth_v1_recommendation: Adapt existing web authentication flows to work within the Capacitor WebView, prioritizing external browser for OAuth and deep links.
must_change_now: Configure Capacitor deep links for OAuth redirects
Verify/adapt internal `window.location` navigation if not using router
Ensure API calls resolve correctly from `capacitor://localhost`
Configure Vite for Capacitor build output
can_defer_until_next_round: Replacing `input type=file` with native Camera/Filesystem plugins
Custom splash screen and app icons
main_risks: Maintaining web vs. mobile code divergence
implementation_order: Initialize Capacitor and configure `capacitor.config.ts` (local assets, deep links)
Refactor `window.location` calls for external redirects using `Browser.open()`
Verify API calls and `credentials: 'include'` in the Capacitor WebView
Test existing `input type=file` for camera/gallery functionality
Build, test, and debug on target iOS and Android devices
